title: chaenorhinum rubrifolium f. rupestre
intro: Chaenorhinum rubrifolium f. rupestre, also known as the rock-toadflax, is a subspecies of the Chaenorhinum rubrifolium plant, a flowering plant in the borage family. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which may help to relieve congestion and ease breathing. Traditionally, rock-toadflax has been prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, which can be consumed to harness its potential health benefits.
